evals.prompt: 幫我根據這份 PRD 拆成 implementation plan：evals/files/login-redesign-prd.md
evals.expected_output: A phased implementation plan that starts with durable architectural decisions, then breaks the work into thin tracer-bullet vertical slices. The plan should cover all PRD user stories, sequence phases by dependency, and avoid horizontal slicing such as 'backend first, frontend later'.
expectations: Plan includes a durable architectural decisions header derived from the PRD
All PRD user stories are mapped to at least one phase
Each phase is a vertical slice spanning end-to-end behavior, not a horizontal layer-only phase
Phases are thin, demoable, and ordered by dependency
Plan does NOT include brittle implementation details such as file names, function names, or low-level call sequences

assertions.name: Has durable architectural decisions header
assertions.criteria: Plan begins with a section identifying durable architectural decisions (route patterns, schema shapes, auth approach, data models) before listing phases

assertions.name: All PRD user stories mapped to phases
assertions.criteria: Every user story from the PRD (P0: single-screen login, social sign-in, magic link, error messages; P1: fallback path, resend guidance, risk checks; P2: remember-me) is covered by at least one phase

assertions.name: Phases are vertical slices not horizontal layers
assertions.criteria: Each phase cuts through multiple layers (e.g. schema + API + UI + tests), not organized as 'backend phase' then 'frontend phase' then 'testing phase'

assertions.name: Phases are thin, demoable, and dependency-ordered
assertions.criteria: Each phase produces a demoable or verifiable increment. Phases are ordered so earlier phases don't depend on later ones. Prefer many thin slices over few thick ones

assertions.name: No brittle implementation details
assertions.criteria: Plan does NOT contain specific file names, function names, class names, or low-level API call sequences. It uses durable decisions (route paths, schema shapes, model names) instead
